purpose: Define retry-safe, stage-bounded transaction semantics for future governed MetaBlooms stages.
problems_addressed: dirty-tree loops caused by uncommitted receipts
re-running a repair in a different root
restoring older releases and losing later work

transaction_phases.purpose: Verify active root, git cleanliness, required handoff, pointer, DAG, ledger, and auto-runaway scope.
Write candidate artifacts only inside staging root unless the stage is explicitly contract-only and already clean.
Run syntax, schema, artifact-existence, content-hash, route, or proof checks required by the contract.
Promote verified outputs into active root using the smallest valid change set.
Commit promoted outputs and receipts to Git.
Update current working pointer and stage-state ledger after commit succeeds.

promotion_rules: Never promote from staging unless all required checks pass.
Promotion must be the smallest valid change set.
Promotion must not delete tracked files unless a specific deletion contract exists.
For contract-only stages, direct writes are allowed only after clean preflight and must be committed in the same stage.
rollback_rules: If preflight fails, do not mutate active root.
If staged verification fails, leave active root unchanged and write a blocked receipt outside active root or commit a blocked receipt by rule F6.
If promotion fails, stop and write a blocker receipt identifying promoted/unpromoted paths.
If commit fails, stop and do not update pointer or ledger.
idempotency_rules: Stage IDs and artifact names must be deterministic.
Re-running a completed stage must overwrite the same target artifacts or detect that they already satisfy the contract.
Receipt filenames for canonical stage receipts must be stable; timestamped receipts are allowed only inside evidence folders.
State updates occur only after a successful commit.
receipt_rules: Every phase must be represented in the final stage receipt.
Blocked receipts must not advance latest_pass.
Success receipts must include outputs, commit, git status, next improvement task, remaining work, and next command.
No success claim is valid without a committed receipt or explicit blocker receipt.
state_update_rules: After commit, update CURRENT_WORKING_BASELINE_POINTER_v1.json to the new commit.
After commit, append PASS entry to STAGE_STATE_LEDGER_v1.json.
Do not update pointer or latest_pass after FAIL/BLOCK.
F6 will define blocked-receipt commit behavior in detail.
non_goals: F3 does not implement a Python runner.
F3 does not export a baseline.
F3 does not perform restore proof.
